Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A new C++ back end for ocamlc 70 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Show HN: Git bayesect – Bayesian Git bisection for non-deterministic bugs 198 upvotes, 26 comments
  3. Fast and Gorgeous Erosion Filter 55 upvotes, 7 comments
  4. Montana referendum to outlaw corporate campaign contributions [video] 21 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. The revenge of the data scientist 87 upvotes, 16 comments
  6. Jax's true calling: Ray-Marching renderers on WebGL 53 upvotes, 8 comments
  7. Ariane 6 user's manual [pdf] 32 upvotes, 4 comments
  8. How-to guide: Commissioning a Sensor Physics R&D Lab 30 upvotes, 4 comments
  9. SolveSpace (open source 2D/3D CAD) working on Windows 2000 (2025) 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  10. Set the Line Before It's Crossed 22 upvotes, 5 comments
  11. StepFun 3.5 Flash is #1 cost-effective model for OpenClaw tasks (300 battles) 139 upvotes, 60 comments
  12. Scientists crack a 20-year nuclear mystery behind the creation of gold 48 upvotes, 17 comments
  13. Windows 95 defenses against installers that overwrite a file with an older one 112 upvotes, 57 comments
  14. ZomboCom stolen by a hacker, sold, now replaced with AI-generated makeover 32 upvotes, 13 comments
  15. The Windows equivalents of the most used Linux commands 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  16. EmDash – A spiritual successor to WordPress that solves plugin security 459 upvotes, 329 comments
  17. Signing data structures the wrong way 69 upvotes, 40 comments
  18. Show HN: Flight-Viz – 10K flights on a 3D globe in 3.5MB of Rust+WASM 57 upvotes, 33 comments
  19. DRAM pricing is killing the hobbyist SBC market 282 upvotes, 209 comments
  20. Swappa.com for GrapheneOS compatible devices – Stay Away 86 upvotes, 55 comments
  21. AI for American-produced cement and concrete 144 upvotes, 107 comments
  22. Ask HN: Who is hiring? (April 2026) 196 upvotes, 159 comments
  23. NASA’s Artemis II Crew Launches to the Moon 598 upvotes, 542 comments
  24. Show HN: Dull – Instagram Without Reels, YouTube Without Shorts (iOS) 36 upvotes, 22 comments
  25. Show HN: Zerobox – Sandbox any command with file, network, credential controls 104 upvotes, 82 comments
  26. IPv6 address, as a sentence you can remember 21 upvotes, 14 comments
  27. Obfuscation is not security – AI can deobfuscate any minified JavaScript code 29 upvotes, 24 comments
  28. SpaceX files to go public 187 upvotes, 243 comments
  29. Ask HN: Who wants to be hired? (April 2026) 54 upvotes, 134 comments
  30. InspectMind AI (YC W24) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 9:30am, 2 April 2026. Source here.
